free_text: Nuclear reactions of tantalum with 3.65 A GeV C-12
ions and 3.65 GeV protons.
Targets stacks containing Mylar, aluminium and tantalum
foils of high purity (99.99 %) were irradiated
with 3.65 A*GeV C-12-ions and 3.65 GeV protons in an
external beam of the Dubna Synchophasotron.
The targets consisted of three tantalum foils (of
thickness 62, 154 and 62mg/cm2), each surrounded front
and back by Mylar catcher foils of thickness 35 mg/cm2.
The target stacks, guarded with 17.5 mg/cm2 Mylar
foils, were preceded on the upstream side by three
aluminium foils of thickness 20 mg/cm2.
Three Ge(Li) detectors used (28 cm3, 45 cm3 and
50 cm3) with resolutions 2.8 keV, 2.3 keV and 2.5 keV
at 1332.5 keV, respectively.
The uncertainty due to the beam flux monitor
Uncertainty includes
* counting statistics,
* detector efficiency,
* target thickness. (Not include ERR-1).
- By Authors - Target arrangements in the present
experiment made it possible to obtain appropriate
ratios for "thick" (154 mg/cm2) and "thin" (62 mg/cm2)
tantalum targets at 3.65 A GeV energy.
Each quoted cross section is the weighted average of as
many as five separate determinations.
Tbl.1 from Jour. of Physics, G17(1991)675
. Correction according to Memo CP-D/216.
Transfer to usual Reaction Formalism from
Elem-Mass Formalism. Reference is added.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected

in Subents 002 - 114. STATUS updated. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR
ERR-ANALYS updated. MONITOR moved to Subents 002 - 114.
